description: Most teams don't realize this until something gets accidentally changed: out of the box, anyone the team trusts to chat with Runneth can also edit anything Runneth knows. That includes the context Runneth references in conversations and the rules for how it behaves for everyone on the team. Saved feedback like "let's not use the word cheap anymore" can quietly become the rule for everyone, not just the person who said it. This skill lets the admin choose which areas get locked down and who is allowed to change each. A short consultative conversation captures what to protect, then Runneth blocks unauthorized edits and routes change requests through an approval channel. Every save gets stamped with who wrote it. New teammates onboard automatically.

post-install.name: Run the setup conversation
post-install.description: Invokes the skill, which walks the admin through the install conversation, deploys the result, and surfaces a plain-language setup-complete message at the end.

changelog.notes: Swap the Neon-only identity resolver for a local daemon-DB resolver. Motion-web identity now resolves from /daemon/conversation-store/conversations.db via the runtime-injected CONVERSATION_ID, so no NEON_DATABASE_URL or production credentials are needed at install time. Resolution logic matches the compatibility resolver proven on the customer fleet in the June 10-11 rollout. Fail-loud semantics for permissions are unchanged. Refs: 2026-06-11 NEON_DATABASE_URL incident, PDEC-7817.

Customize Write Access refresh on top of v3.0.0. Same six primitives and seven-phase install, with layered-in education, voice, and trim: renamed display title to "Decide who can change Runneth," SKILL heading renamed to "Customize Write Access," new stage-setting intro, and a behavior-protection layer added to the install phases.

Reframe and behavior-protection layer (later refined in 3.1.5). Renamed the skill to Customize Write Access and extended Phase 2/3 outcomes to cover protecting how Runneth behaves, not just what it knows.

Standalone v3 rebuild. Six primitives (people, spaces, writers per space, attribution, approval routing, identity resolution), no mode toggle, seven-phase consultative install. Bundled to land on main as one coherent unit.

Neon-first identity. motion-whoami.sh resolves the speaker email via the Neon agent_conversation table with no SQLite fallback, exiting non-zero on lookup failure so strict permissions never silently fall back to stale data. Renamed workspace-map.json to organization-map.json.
Added a post-install intro so the install ends with a short "what just opened up + try this now" message instead of completing silently.

Initial release under the add-roles-permissions name (renamed from deploy-security-protocol / add-permissions). Admin chooses which areas to lock down and who may change each; unauthorized edits are blocked and routed through an approval channel, every save is attributed, and new teammates onboard automatically.
